description.value: Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.
Used for rolling to hit when fighting in ranged or melee combat, in place of any other skills. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling checks involving feats of physicality, including athletics and evasion. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling when making checks that require training or finesse. Note: if the task is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers accordingly. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.
The Nomad Rocker is a Poor Quality Exotic Very Heavy Pistol that fires rocks. Ammo is free for this weapon. Full rules can be found in Black Chrome on Page 115.
Light version of an Armorjack. No armor penalty.
Light version of an Armorjack for your head. No armor penalty.

A classic, double-barrel shotgun. Hasn't weathered the test of time especially well, but still in demand despite that.

Can only load basic shells. The Boomstick is a standard-quality shotgun with RoF and magazine size of 2. Any turn that both shells are fired, the player only makes one attack roll for both rounds of damage.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 4/Elite Threat - Mid-level, named threats, who run the bulk of activity for most orgs within NC. If a PC didn't choose to become an edgerunner, they probably would've eventually ended up in a position like this instead.

Gang-Wide Ability - Moto Rollers: When a Steel Vaquero is in a scene, they're accompanied by a Generic Compact, Generic Truck, Generic Van, Buggy, or Hauler; each subsequent Vaquero adds an upgrade to the vehicle depending on their purpose in the scene. Once a vehicle has more than half of its seats occupied, you may place another.

Ability - Expert Driver: When a Steel Driver rolls for damage with a vehicle, they always deal at least half the damage the roll is capable of inflicting. They are unable to fail technical checks to drive or repair vehicles, unless such a check is opposed.

Vaquero Drivers are some of the best the nomads have to offer, honing their skills on the narrow highways of the California coastline and in the desert dunes outside of Night City. They're typically honest, unless it comes to their ride - then they'll say pretty much anything.
